title: node:test, Supertest, Contracts, Properties, Fuzzing, and Fault Injection · Inference Brief: Contract Openapi Drift
summary: Tests call live dependencies, assert only status 200, share mutable state, never vary malformed inputs, and mistake mocks or coverage for behavioral proof. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts contract openapi drift increment in layered deterministic server verification matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Verify runtime requests and responses against schemas and consumer cases while detecting undocumented statuses, fields, nullability, security, and behavioral drift.
Exercise Express handlers with Supertest using exact methods, paths, fields, bodies, identity, effects, errors, and cleanup without unnecessary public listeners.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Contract Openapi Drift · Theory Contract Openapi Drift
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 2140 in a accessible status API serving assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate release on layered domain, handler, fuzz, and resource-lifecycle evidence; retain compatibility with one older generated client; then replace an average latency claim with p95 and event-loop saturation evidence. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Verify runtime requests and responses against schemas and consumer cases while detecting undocumented statuses, fields, nullability, security, and behavioral drift.
